SEARCH
Package com.dua3.utility.fx.controls

Class ColumnDefGeneric<S,T>

java.lang.Object
com.dua3.utility.fx.controls.AbstractColumnDef<S,T>
com.dua3.utility.fx.controls.ColumnDefGeneric<S,T>
Type Parameters:
S - The type of the row object from which the column value is derived and to which it is set.
T - The type of the value held by this column.
All Implemented Interfaces:
ColumnDef<S,T>
public final class ColumnDefGeneric<S,T> extends AbstractColumnDef<S,T> implements ColumnDef<S,T>
Represents a generic column definition intended for use in tabular data structures or grids. The column supports customizable behavior for value retrieval, value updates, and rendering using provided functional interfaces.

  • Method Details

    • nodeFactory

      public BiFunction<@Nullable javafx.scene.Node,T,@Nullable javafx.scene.Node> nodeFactory()
      Provides a function that creates or modifies a Node representation of the cell content based on the current value.

      The method returns a BiFunction that takes an optional existing Node and the cell value as input, and outputs a new or updated Node. This function can be used to define how the cell content is rendered or updated dynamically.

      Returns:
      a BiFunction that accepts an optional Node and a cell value, and returns an optional Node.

    • startEdit

      public BiFunction<@Nullable javafx.scene.Node,Consumer<@Nullable T>,@Nullable javafx.scene.Node> startEdit()
      Provides a function to start editing a cell's content by returning a BiFunction. The function takes a Node (the current cell representation) and a Consumer of the updated value. It may return a new Node representation for the cell during the editing process.
      Returns:
      A BiFunction that takes a Node and a Consumer<T> for processing the edited value, and returns an updated Node, or null if no Node is applicable or required.

    • cancelEdit

      public Function<@Nullable javafx.scene.Node,@Nullable javafx.scene.Node> cancelEdit()
      Provides a function to cancel the editing process of a cell.
      Returns:
      A Function that takes the currently edited Node as input and returns the Node to be used after the cancellation process, or null if no specific Node is required.
